example.validation: use the example ValueSet when suitable without treating it as a closed clinical universe

extensible.validation: use the bound ValueSet when it contains an applicable concept; otherwise preserve the outside coding or text and the reason

preferred.validation: prefer the bound ValueSet and retain an auditable outside mapping when needed

required.validation: selected coded answers must validate in the bound ValueSet

projection.data_absent: dataAbsentReason remains separate from the clinical answer ValueSet
projection.generic_binding_preservation: retain the generic SNOMED/local ValueSet as fallback metadata even when an element binding takes precedence
projection.questionnaire: Questionnaire.item.answerValueSet receives the effective canonical and item.type follows binding strength
projection.resource: the same coding is validated again against the mapped target element before resource projection

purpose: Apply the terminology binding of the actual target FHIR R4 element before the platform's generic SNOMED CT answer preference.

scope.application: only facts with an explicit exact or equivalent mapping to a target FHIR element
scope.candidate_targets: discover and report the element binding but do not change answer coding
scope.jurisdictional_overlay: for Korean resource projection, apply an explicitly selected hl7.fhir.kr.core#2.0.0 profile before the base resource binding
scope.resource_registry: all base FHIR R4 resource StructureDefinitions published in profiles-resources.json
scope.source_defined_fixed_questionnaires: excluded unless separately authorized and verified
